Introduction to the MEAN stack
•The MEAN stack is a popular full-stack JavaScript framework used to build dynamic web applications.
•It consists of four main technologies: MongoDB, Express.js, Angular, and Node.js.
Components of the MEAN Stack
•MongoDB: A NoSQL database that stores application data in a flexible, JSON-like format called BSON.
•Express.js: A web application framework for Node.js designed to build robust APIs and web applications
quickly.
•Angular: A front-end framework maintained by Google, used to create interactive and dynamic web
interfaces.
•Node.js: A JavaScript runtime environment that allows developers to run JavaScript on the server side.

[Page 14]
Architecture of MEAN Stack
•The client sends a request through the browser, like clicking a button or submitting a form.
•AngularJS sends the request to the server to fetch or store data.
•Node.js receives and handles the request coming from AngularJS.
•Node.js forwards the request to Express.js for further processing.
•Express.js communicates with MongoDB to get or store the required data.
•MongoDB retrieves the data and sends it back to Express.js.
•Express.js returns the data to the Node.js server.
•Node.js sends the response back to AngularJS.
•AngularJS displays the received data or result to the client in the browser.
•The overall flow is: Client → AngularJS → Node.js → Express.js → MongoDB → Express.js → Node.js →
AngularJS → Client.

Benefits of Using MEAN Stack
•Full JavaScript Stack: Developers can use JavaScript throughout the entire application, reducing
context-switching and making code more reusable and maintainable.
•Modern & Scalable: Each component is designed for performance and scalability, making MEAN
suitable for building cloud-ready and enterprise-grade web applications.
•Efficient Development: With a unified language, teams can collaborate more effectively and new
developers can ramp up quickly.

It picks up one paper, finishes it (or sends it to someone else), then picks up the next one.

•Synchronous
•Tasks happen step by step
•Next line runs only after the previous line finishes

JavaScript Runtime Architecture
•JavaScript is single-threaded - it executes one line at a time.
•But your app needs to do multiple things without blocking the page:
•Wait for API responses
•Handle button clicks
•Wait for timers
•Process promises
•Perform async operations
JavaScript achieves this using:
•Call Stack
•Web APIs / Node APIs
•Callback Queue (Task Queue)
•Microtask Queue (Promise Queue)
•Event Loop

[Page 19]
Event Loop
The Event Loop is like a traffic police officer who controls the flow of JavaScript tasks.
What it does
1. Keeps checking if the call stack is empty
•The call stack is where JavaScript runs your code.
•If it’s busy → event loop waits
•If it becomes empty → event loop sends new tasks
2. First sends tasks from the Microtask Queue
•Microtasks = Promises, async/await (These are treated as VIP tasks.)
•Event loop always runs them first.
3. Then sends tasks from the Callback Queue
•Callback Queue = setTimeout, setInterval, DOM events (These are normal tasks.)
•They run only after all microtasks are finished.

- Q. 10 Explain the configuration files used in MongoDB.
- MongoDB uses a configuration file to control how the MongoDB server runs. The default
- configuration file is mongod.conf.
- Purpose of mongod.conf
- e Defines server settings and behavior
- e Helps in customizing MongoDB without command-line options
- Important Configuration Options
- Storage: database path (dbPath)
- System Log: log file location
- Network: port number and IP binding
- Security: authentication and authorization
- Replication: replica set settings

- Reduces disk I/O operations

- Q. 20 What aggregation pipelines in MongoDB?
- are
- Solution
- MongoDB uses aggregation pipelines to process, transform, and analyze data stored in
- collections. An aggregation pipeline is a step-by-step data processing framework where
- documents pass through multiple stages, and each stage performs a specific operation.
- Think of it like an assembly line:
- data enters — gets filtered — grouped — transformed — final result comes out.
- Why Aggregation Pipelines Are Used
- To perform data analysis and reporting
- e
- e To transform raw data into meaningful results
- To perform operations similar to SQL GROUP BY
- e To process large datasets efficiently
- How Aggregation Pipeline Works
- e Data flows through a sequence of stages
- e Each stage processes the output of the previous stage
- e The final stage returns the result

- 1. $match
- Filters documents (similar to WHERE in SQL).

- Best Practices
- Design Based on Application Queries
- Structure data according to how the application reads and writes data.
- Use Embedding When Possible
- Embed related data for faster read performance.
- Use Referencing for Large Shared Data
- or
- Reference documents when data is large or frequently reused.
- Avoid Deeply Nested Documents
- Keep document depth minimal for better performance.
- Optimize Index Usage
- Create indexes on frequently queried fields.
- Balance Data Duplication
- Allow controlled duplication to improve read speed.
- Plan for Scalability
- Design schema keeping future data growth in mind.

Node.js

Instead

usage

performance.

function

- and server using standard HTTP methods. In Express.js, RESTful APIs are created to handle
- HTTP requests and perform CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) operations on resources.
- REST is based on the concept of resources, which are represented using URLs (Uniform
- Resource Locators). Each is identified by unique endpoint.

- RESTful APIs are stateless, meaning each request from the client must contain all necessary

11
What
is
authentication
and

Solution
Authentication
and
Authorization
are
two
important
security
concepts
used
in
web
applications
to
protect
data
and
resources.
Authentication
is
the
process
of
verifying
the
identity
of
a
user.
It
checks
whether
the
user
is
genuine
or
not.
This
is
usually
done
by
asking
for
credentials
such
as
a
username
and
password,
OTP,
or
biometric
verification.
If
the
provided
details
are
correct,
the
user
is
successfully
authenticated.
For
example,
when
a
user
logs
into
an
application
using
email
and
password,
the
system
verifies
the
credentials
before
allowing
access.
Authorization,
on
the
other
hand,
is
the
process
of
determining
what
an
authenticated
user
is
allowed
to
do.
It
defines
the
permissions
or
access
rights
of
a
user.
After
authentication,
the
system
checks
whether
the
user
has
permission
to
access
a
specific
resource
or
perform
a
particular
action.
For
example,
in
an
admin
panel:
●
An
admin
can
add,
update,
and
delete
users.
●
A
normal
user
can
only
view
their
profile.
In
simple
terms:
●
Authentication
answers
the
question:
“Who
are
you?”
●
Authorization
answers
the
question:
“What
are
you
allowed
to
do?”
Thus,
authentication
verifies
identity,
while
authorization
controls
access,
and
both
are
essential
for
securing
web
applications.

- Q 14 What are sessions in Express.js?
- Solution
- Sessions in Express.js are used to store user-specific data on the server side across multiple
- HTTP requests. Since HTTP is a stateless protocol, each request is independent. Sessions help
- maintain user state, such as login information, shopping cart data, or user preferences.

- JWT (JSON Web Token) is token-based authentication mechanism used in Node.js
- a
- applications to securely transmit information between client and server. It is commonly used for
- stateless authentication in RESTful APIs.
- A JWT consists of three parts separated by dots (.):
- e Header — Contains the token type and signing algorithm.
- e Payload Contains user data (such as user ID, email, role).
- —
- e Signature — Ensures the token is secure and not tampered with, created using a secret
- key.
- The structure looks like:
- Header.Payload.Signature
- Working of JWT Authentication:
- 4 User Login:
- The user sends login credentials (username and password) to the server.
- 2. Token Generation:
- If credentials are valid, the server generates a JWT using a secret key and sends it back to the
- client.
- 3. Token Storage:
- The client stores the token (usually in localStorage or cookies).
- 4. Accessing Protected Routes:
- For subsequent requests, the client sends the token in the Authorization header:
- Authorization: Bearer <token>
- 5. Token Verification:
- The server verifies the token using the secret key.
- e If valid — access is granted.
- e If invalid or expired — access is denied.
- Advantages of JWT:
- Stateless (no need to store sessions the server)
- e on
- e Scalable for large applications
- Secure and compact
- e
- Suitable for REST APIs and mobile applications
- Thus, JWT authentication in Node.js provides a secure and efficient way to manage user
- authentication without maintaining server-side sessions.

Explain Angular framework. What are its key features and advantages in web development? Solution Angular Framework Angular is a TypeScript-based open-source front-end web application framework developed and maintained by Google. It is used to build dynamic, scalable, and single-page web applications (SPAs). Angular provides a structured approach to building web applications by dividing the application into components, modules, and services, which makes development easier and more organized. Angular mainly uses HTML, CSS, and TypeScript to create interactive user interfaces. Architecture of Angular The Angular framework follows a component-based architecture. Main building blocks include: ● Components – Control the view and UI logic. ● Modules (NgModule) – Organize the application into logical parts. ● Templates – HTML used to display data. ● Services – Provide reusable business logic and data handling. ● Directives – Extend HTML functionality. ● Dependency Injection – Manage service objects efficiently. Solution Angular Component An Angular Component is the basic building block of an application developed using the Angular framework. A component controls a part of the user interface (UI) and defines how that part of the application behaves. Each Angular application is made up of multiple components that work together to build the complete user interface. For example: ● Header component ● Login component ● Dashboard component ● Footer component Each component manages its own logic, template, and style. Explain the lifecycle hooks of Angular components. Solution Lifecycle Hooks of Angular Components In Angular, Lifecycle Hooks are special methods that allow developers to execute code at different stages of a component's life. These stages include creation, update, and destruction of a component. Lifecycle hooks help developers control and manage component behavior during its lifecycle. Angular Component Lifecycle Phases The lifecycle of a component mainly consists of three phases: 1. Creation Phase – Component is initialized. 2. Change Detection Phase – Angular checks and updates data. 3. Destruction Phase – Component is removed from the application. Important Angular Lifecycle Hooks Lifecycle Hook Description ngOnChanges() Called when input properties change. ngOnInit() Called once after the component is initialized. ngDoCheck() Called during every change detection cycle. ngAfterContentInit() Called after external content is projected into the component. ngAfterContentChecked() Called after projected content is checked. ngAfterViewInit() Called after the component’s view and child views are initialized. Example @NgModule({ providers: [AuthService, LoggerService] }) export class CoreModule { } Services Q 13. What is an Angular Service? Explain why services are used in Angular applications. Solution Angular Service In Angular, a Service is a class used to perform specific tasks such as business logic, data sharing, and communication with external APIs. Services help keep the application organized by separating data handling and business logic from the user interface components. Services are commonly used together with Dependency Injection, which allows Angular to provide service instances to different components automatically. Purpose of Angular Services The main purpose of services is to provide reusable functionality that can be shared across multiple components in an Angular application. Instead of writing the same code in different components, developers create a service and reuse it wherever needed. Explain Dependency Injection in Angular with an example. Solution Dependency Injection in Angular In Angular, Dependency Injection (DI) is a design pattern used to provide required objects (dependencies) to a class instead of creating them inside the class. Angular has a built-in dependency injection system that automatically supplies services or objects to components and other classes. In simple terms, Dependency Injection allows Angular to create and manage service objects and inject them where they are needed. Why Dependency Injection is Used Dependency Injection is used in Angular to: ● Reduce tight coupling between classes ● Improve code reusability ● Make applications easier to test and maintain ● Share services across multiple components Instead of creating objects manually, Angular injects them automatically. Explain Data Binding in Angular. Describe its types with examples. Solution Data Binding in Angular is the mechanism that connects the data of the component (TypeScript code) with the view (HTML template). It allows automatic synchronization of data between the model (component) and the view (UI). In simple terms, data binding helps Angular display data in the UI and update it when the data changes. Why Data Binding is Important ● Reduces manual DOM manipulation. ● Automatically updates UI when data changes. ● Makes applications dynamic and interactive. ● Improves code readability and maintainability. Types of Data Binding in Angular Angular provides four main types of data binding. 1. Interpolation 2. Property Binding 3. Event Binding 4.     [Page 84] Forms and Validation Q 18. Explain Template-driven forms in Angular. Solution Template-Driven Forms in Angular In Angular, Template-driven forms are a type of form where most of the form logic and validation are handled in the HTML template. Angular uses directives to create and manage forms directly in the template. Template-driven forms are simple and suitable for small applications or simple forms because they require less coding in TypeScript. They mainly rely on the FormsModule and the ngModel directive for two-way data binding. - Q 21. What is Angular Routing? Explain how navigation works in Angular applications.
- Solution
- Angular Routing
- In Angular, Routing is a mechanism that allows users to navigate between different views or
- components of an application without reloading the entire page. Angular routing is used to build
- Single Page Applications (SPAs) where only a part of the page changes when the user
- navigates.
- Routing helps developers map URLs to specific components, enabling smooth navigation
- between different pages such as Home, About, Contact, etc.
- Example URLs:
- /home
- /about
- /contact
- Each URL displays a different component.

Explain the process of configuring routes in Angular. Solution Configuring Routes in Angular In Angular, routing configuration is the process of defining how different URL paths map to specific components in an application. It allows users to navigate between views without reloading the page, which is a key feature of Single Page Applications (SPA). Routing is handled by the RouterModule, which manages navigation and displays components based on the URL. Steps to Configure Routes in Angular 1. Create Components First, create the components that will be used for navigation. Example components: ● HomeComponent ● AboutComponent ● ContactComponent Using Angular CLI: ng generate component home ng generate component about ng generate component contact 2. Import RouterModule Import RouterModule and define routes inside a routing module.

Explain the HttpClient module in Angular and its role in API communication. Solution HttpClient Module in Angular In Angular, the HttpClient module is used to communicate with backend servers and APIs. It allows Angular applications to send HTTP requests and receive data from external sources such as databases or REST APIs. The HttpClient module is part of @angular/common/http and provides methods to perform operations such as: ● GET (retrieve data) ● POST (send data) ● PUT (update data) ● DELETE (remove data) These operations help Angular applications interact with backend technologies such as Node.js, Express, or databases. Role of HttpClient in API Communication The HttpClient module plays an important role in connecting the frontend Angular application with backend services. It is used to: ● Fetch data from APIs ● Send user input data to servers ● Update existing records ● Delete records from databases Example: retrieving user data from an API. Step 1: Import HttpClientModule First, import HttpClientModule in the main module.

- Observables can emit multiple values over time, unlike promises which return only one value.
- 3. Easy Data Stream Handling
- They help manage streams of data such as real-time updates, WebSocket data, or events.

- e merge

- Solution
- Role of RESTful APIs in Integrating Angular with Backend Services
- In modern web applications, the frontend built using Angular communicates with backend
- services using RESTful APIs. The backend is often developed using technologies such as
- Express.js running on Node.js and connected to databases like MongoDB.
- A RESTful API (Representational State Transfer API) is web service that allows different
- a
- systems to communicate with each other using HTTP requests and responses.
- RESTful APIs bridge between the Angular frontend and the backend
- act as a server.
- Role of RESTful APIs
- RESTful APIs enable Angular applications to send and receive data from backend systems.
- They allow the frontend to perform operations such as:
- e Fetching data from the server
- e Sending user input data
- e Updating existing records
- e Deleting data from the database
- This allows Angular applications become dynamic and data-driven.

Explain how Angular services are used to consume Express.js APIs. Solution Angular Services to Consume Express.js APIs In a full-stack web application, the frontend developed using Angular communicates with backend APIs created using Express.js running on Node.js. Angular services are used to send HTTP requests to the backend APIs and retrieve data. Services help keep API communication separate from the component logic, making the application more organized and maintainable. Role of Angular Services in API Communication Angular services are used to: ● Send HTTP requests to backend APIs ● Retrieve data from the server ● Send user data to the backend ● Handle business logic and data processing Instead of writing API calls in every component, developers create a single service that handles all API communication. Step 1: Create an Express.js API First, create an API in the backend server. Example: const express = require('express' ); const app = express(); app.get('/api/students' , (req, res) => { res.json([ { name: "Suraj", course: "CSE" }, { name: "Rahul", course: "IT" } ]); }); app.listen(3000); This API returns student data in JSON format. Step 2: Import HttpClientModule in Angular Angular uses the HttpClient module to communicate with APIs. app.module.ts [DIAGRAM LABELS] - Advantages of Angular—Node Integration - e Enables full-stack application development - Allows separation of frontend and backend - e - e Supports real-time and dynamic applications - Improves scalability and maintainability - Q 5.

Solution Authentication in Web Applications Authentication is the process of verifying the identity of a user before allowing access to an application or system. It ensures that only authorized users can access certain resources or perform specific actions. In modern web applications, authentication is commonly implemented using technologies such as login forms, tokens, sessions, or OAuth systems. - Importance of Authentication in Angular Applications
- Authentication plays a critical role in Angular applications for several
- reasons.
- 1. Security
- Authentication ensures that only verified users can access the application, preventing
- unauthorized access.
- 2. Access Control
- It allows developers to restrict access to certain pages or features based on user identity.
- Example:
- Admin dashboard
- e
- e User profile page
- 3. Route Protection
- Angular applications can protect routes using route guards to ensure that only authenticated
- users can navigate to certain pages.
- /login — accessible to everyone
- /dashboard — accessible only to logged- users
- 4. Data Protection
- Authentication ensures that sensitive user data is only available to authorized users.
- Banking information
- e Personal user details

- Solution
- Authorization and Role-Based Access Control in Angular Applications
- In web applications, authorization is the of determining what actions a is allowed to
- process user
- perform after authentication. While authentication verifies the identity of a user, authorization
- ensures that the user can only access resources that they are permitted to use.
- In applications built with Angular, authorization is commonly implemented using Role-Based
- Access Control (RBAC). In RBAC, users are assigned roles such as Admin, Manager, or User,
- and access to different parts of the application is controlled based on these roles.
- Concept of Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
- Role-Based Access Control works by assigning permissions according to user roles.
- Example:
- Role Access Permission
- Admin Full access to system
- Manager Access to reports and management tools
- User Access to personal profile and limited features
- Implementation Steps in Angular
- 1. Authenticate the User
- First, the user logs in through the Angular application. The backend server verifies the
- credentials and returns a token containing user information and role.
- Example token payload:
- " admin"
- Angular stores this token in localStorage or sessionStorage.
- 2. Store User Role in Angular
- After login, Angular extracts the role from the authentication
- response.
- localStorage etItem( » respons ole);
- The role will be used to control access to routes and features.

- Q 13. What are WebSockets? Explain how they are used for real-time communication in
- web applications.
- Solution
- WebSockets
- WebSockets are a communication protocol that enables real-time, two-way (bidirectional)
- communication between a client and a server over a single, persistent connection. Unlike
- traditional HTTP requests, WebSockets allow data to be sent and received instantly without
- repeatedly opening new connections.
- WebSockets are widely used in modern applications built with frameworks such as Angular on
- the frontend and servers like Node.js or Express.js on the backend.

- Error Handling in Angular Applications
- Error handling is the of detecting, managing, and responding that during
- process to errors occur
- the execution of an application. In applications built with Angular, error handling helps ensure
- that the application smoothly, prevents crashes, and provides meaningful feedback to
- runs
- users.
- Errors may occur due to various reasons such as invalid user input, server failures, network
- issues, or programming mistakes. Angular provides several mechanisms to detect and manage
- these errors effectively.
- Types of Errors in Angular
- Angular applications commonly encounter the following types of errors:
- Error Type Description
- Syntax Errors Errors caused by incorrect code syntax
- Runtime Errors Errors occurring during application execution
- HTTP Errors Errors related to API requests
- Logical Errors Errors due to incorrect program logic
- Methods of Error Handling in Angular
- Angular provides different techniques for handling errors.
